title: Savoury Tart
excerpt: My mom often made savoury tart for us on a Saturday. This was before the days of quiche. You can replace the little sausages with corned beef if you feel like it. My mom used tinned viennas – that works too. Ingredients 3 eggs 375 ml milk 30 ml …
html:
My mom often made savoury tart for us on a Saturday. This was before the days of quiche. You can replace the little sausages with corned beef if you feel like it. My mom used tinned viennas – that works too.

Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • 375 ml milk
  • 30 ml cake flour
  • 6 vienna sausages or 1 tin vienna sausages sliced into rounds
  • 200 g bacon bits
  • 250 ml grated cheese
  • 1 onion, grated
  • 10 ml mustard powder
  • salt and pepper
  • 100 ml finely chopped parsley

Method

  1. Fry the onion and bacon in butter.
  2. Add the vienna sausages and fry a little, then let it cool
  3. Whisk the eggs and milk with a balloon whisk.
  4. Whisk in the flour and mustard powder.
  5. Stir in the cheese.
  6. Stir the onion/bacon and sausage mixture into the egg mixture.
  7. If you're using bacon, be careful with the salt, as bacon is very salty. Otherwise salt and pepper to taste.
  8. Stir in the finely chopped parsley.
  9. Pour into a greased dish and bake at 180C for 30 – 40 minutes.
  10. The tart can be eaten cold or warm.
